attack_rows.cost_model: O(N^2) direct comparisons, or O(N log N) with FFT correlation under full-table access

attack_rows.cost_model: O(N log N + NK) with full-table FFT and K scored frequencies
attack_rows.notes: Recovers cyclic shifts from Fourier phase ratios when full-table access is allowed.

attack_rows.cost_model: O(N log N) transform plus top_k=10 phase-ratio scoring
attack_rows.notes: Tests whether a small Fourier support already classically reveals the shift.

attack_rows.cost_model: O(sample_count * |G|) sparse-overlap scoring with sample_count=15 random f-samples and g-samples
attack_rows.notes: Models a random-sample adversary that cannot query arbitrary shifted locations.

attack_rows.cost_model: O(|G| * q) chosen evaluator queries with q=15; exponential in n unless the shift space has additional exploitable structure
attack_rows.notes: Arbitrary evaluator access plus an exhaustive shift scan recovers many families; this is a model-separation baseline, not a polynomial-time dequantization.

query_model_rows.notes: Full-table access is sufficient to recover nondegenerate hidden shifts and should not be counted as quantum evidence.
A legal random-sample baseline recovered the shift; this family cannot support the sampled-access claim.
Arbitrary point queries plus exhaustive scoring recover the shift, but the query count still scales with |G|.

Full-table access is sufficient to recover nondegenerate hidden shifts and should not be counted as quantum evidence.
The sampled budget reaches the collision scale but current random-sample baselines still fail.
Arbitrary point queries plus exhaustive scoring recover the shift, but the query count still scales with |G|.
